title: Fatal error when invoked with multiple identical arguments
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

WIth podman-3.x and earlier, podman would accept multiple `--network` arguments, and act upon the final value specified.

WIth podman-4.0.0 release candidates, podman will now abort with the error:

```
Error: cannot set multiple networks without bridge network mode, selected mode host: invalid argument
```

… _even if the repeated argument provides the same value_!

e.g. `podman run … --network host … --network host …` is now a fatal error.

I can accept specifying two different values being an error (although personally I'd likely retain the previous behaviour of acting on the last value specified but now also issuing a warning) - but making a duplicate argument fatal feels a little over-prescriptive...

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.0-rc3
API Version:  4.0.0-rc3
Go Version:   go1.17.5
Git Commit:   9b441c0dda57a79dc96eba90be6e2d1ece4b4905
Built:        Sat Jan 29 19:50:32 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


Yes

comments.body:
With podman 4.0 you can set multiple networks with static ips/macs, that is why we changed the flag to an array.
I honestly do not understand why you would want to use `--network bridge --network host` and only host should be used. This looks like a terrible user experience since we silently ignore the first argument. I am aware that this is how it worked with previous versions but I still dislike it.

To confirm, this isn't actually above trying to use dissimilar arguments - it's for cases where a command-line is assembled, and previously prior arguments didn't have to be considered (e.g. `--network host --network none` would be interpreted as `--network none`) - but I understand why this may have been tightened-up.

It still feels as if `--network host --network host` should be non-fatal, though - by a standard reading of `getopt()`-style argument handling you'd expect this to be interpreted as a single assignation of host to the network option (as opposed to something like `--network host,host` or similar).
Feel free to open a PR I do not have time to work on this.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I don't see this as a bug or issue, so I am going to close it.  If  you want to open a PR to allow duplicate --network options then feel free to do so.
